name: stack_parts
description: Kit-bash assembly: stack an ordered chain of mesh parts tip-to-tail along a shared axis so their connection faces butt together collinearly. Uses end-cap centroids projected on the axis (robust to gear teeth / knurling / bolt detail that defeat ring-based joint detectors like measure_joints). The first part stays put (shoulder/base end); each subsequent part is translated so its near cap-center meets the previous part's far cap-center, offset by gap (negative = insertion overlap, e.g. seating a dowel into a socket). Optionally rotates each part so its principal axis aligns to the target axis first (off by default — PCA axis is unreliable for chunky/cube-ish parts). Returns the resolved axis, growth direction, and per-part displacement.

names.description: Ordered chain of mesh object names, shoulder/base end first.

axis.description: Optional world-space stacking axis [x,y,z]. Default: principal axis of the most elongated part in the chain.

gap.description: Spacing between consecutive cap centers along the axis. Negative inserts/overlaps (seat a dowel/peg into a socket). Default 0.

align_axes.description: Rotate each part so its principal axis matches the target axis before stacking. Leave false for chunky parts whose PCA axis is unreliable. Default false.

frac.description: Fraction of part length used for each end-cap slice when computing cap centroids. Default 0.12.

author: modeler-agent
